selection.selection_note: Judged only owned repair batch 37 against owned_repair_batch37 metadata, full standard_source_table semantic/source metadata where available, S-57/OpenCPN portrayal instructions, S-101 exact SVG witnesses, local OpenCPN reference paths listed in the catalog, Aqua Map metadata where listed, and actual batch-37 repaired SVGs on the branch.

verdicts.observed: Candidate has the radio circle and two question marks, but it keeps a continuous magenta diamond enclosure around the circle.
verdicts.expected: RCLDEF01 must be the unknown-direction radio calling-in point: central magenta circle, left/right question marks, and separate upper/lower direction chevron witnesses rather than a closed diamond sign.
verdicts.judge_comments: The unknown-direction meaning is partly present, but the repaired silhouette still does not match the S-101/OpenCPN witness closely enough. The closed diamond changes the load-bearing radio-call silhouette and repeats the prior diamond-placeholder problem in a subtler form. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw RCLDEF01 with the central circle, two question marks, and separate upper/lower V direction witnesses; remove the continuous diamond side enclosure.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:radio calling-in point; SY(RCLDEF01); traffic/radio line context may add LS(DASH,1,TRFCD) and OBJNAM text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a magenta circle inside a closed diamond, with no single one-way triangular direction witness.
verdicts.expected: RDOCAL02 must be the one-direction radio calling-in point: central circle plus one triangular/V direction cue only.
verdicts.judge_comments: The radio circle is present, but the closed diamond adds a second implied direction and removes the specific one-way call-in cue. This is a navigation semantics failure, not a style difference. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw RDOCAL02 as a central radio-call circle with one upper triangular/V direction point; remove the closed diamond and any opposite-direction cue.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:radio calling-in point for traffic in one direction only; SY(RDOCAL02,ORIENT); optional OBJNAM/COMCHA text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a magenta circle inside a closed diamond with a vertical center stroke.
verdicts.expected: RDOCAL03 must be the bidirectional radio calling-in point: central circle with opposed upper and lower triangular/V direction witnesses.
verdicts.judge_comments: The center stroke does not encode the required bidirectional radio-call symbol. The closed diamond remains a wrong sign-like enclosure and the two opposed direction witnesses are not preserved. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw RDOCAL03 as the central circle with separate upper and lower triangular/V direction witnesses; remove the closed diamond and vertical center stroke.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:radio calling-in point for traffic in both directions; SY(RDOCAL03,ORIENT); optional OBJNAM/COMCHA text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a simple magenta circular radio-station point and avoids mast, broadcast-wave, or radar-station substitutions.
verdicts.expected: RDOSTA02 is the radio station symbol: a simple magenta circle/point witness.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: correct radio-station family, colour, and circular shape. Scale/stroke are Helm-styled but do not change the symbol meaning. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:radio station; SY(RDOSTA02); optional COMCHA/DGPS text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a grey recommended-track witness with horizontal track segments, opposed side chevrons, and a central question-mark unknown-direction cue.
verdicts.expected: RECDEF51 is recommended track as an area, direction not defined in data: split track line, central unknown-direction question mark, and non-directional chevron context.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: the prior directional-arrow treatment is gone and the unknown-direction cue is present. Stroke weight/text scale are Helm style differences only. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:recommended track as area, direction not defined; SY(RECDEF51); area boundary may use LS(DASH,1,CHGRD) or LC(NAVARE51).

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a grey vertical recommended-track line with two opposed chevrons and no standalone horizontal arrow sign.
verdicts.expected: RECTRC55 is the two-way recommended track as an area, not based on fixed marks: vertical track witness with opposed direction chevrons and broken/non-fixed-mark track segments.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: two-way route meaning, grey track colour, opposed chevrons, and non-fixed-mark broken track context are preserved. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:recommended two-way track as area, not based on fixed marks; SY(RECTRC55,ORIENT); optional ORIENT text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate uses the two-way chevron track but keeps broken/non-fixed-mark line segments and adds a central circle.
verdicts.expected: RECTRC56 must be the fixed-mark two-way recommended-track witness: continuous track line through the opposed chevrons, without an added circle marker.
verdicts.judge_comments: The two-way direction is recognizable, but the fixed-mark distinction is not preserved. The central circle and broken line make it drift from the S-101/OpenCPN fixed-mark witness and too close to a custom marker rather than the fixed-mark route symbol. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw RECTRC56 with a continuous vertical fixed-mark track line and opposed chevrons; remove the added center circle and broken-line treatment.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:recommended two-way track as area, based on fixed marks; SY(RECTRC56,ORIENT); optional ORIENT text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a grey one-way recommended-track witness with a single chevron on the track axis and no standalone horizontal dashed arrow.
verdicts.expected: RECTRC57 is recommended one-way track as an area, not based on fixed marks: broken/non-fixed-mark track line and one direction chevron on the track axis.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: one-way direction, grey track colour, and non-fixed-mark broken track context are preserved. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:recommended one-way track as area, not based on fixed marks; SY(RECTRC57,ORIENT); optional ORIENT text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate uses the one-way chevron track but keeps broken/non-fixed-mark line segments and adds a central circle.
verdicts.expected: RECTRC58 must be the fixed-mark one-way recommended-track witness: continuous track line with one direction chevron, without an added circle marker.
verdicts.judge_comments: The one-way route direction is recognizable, but the fixed-mark semantics are not. The central circle is not in the S-101/OpenCPN witness and the broken line makes it too close to RECTRC57/custom marker treatment. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: Redraw RECTRC58 with a continuous vertical fixed-mark track line and one direction chevron; remove the added center circle and broken-line treatment.
source_refs_used: s57_structure:recommended one-way track as area, based on fixed marks; SY(RECTRC58,ORIENT); optional ORIENT text.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a magenta retro-reflector comb/E witness with one vertical spine and three horizontal bars.
verdicts.expected: RETRFL01 is retro reflector, paper chart: magenta comb/E witness with vertical spine and three horizontal bars.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: correct reflector family, magenta colour, and comb/E geometry. Candidate is Helm-scaled but preserves the symbol semantics. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a magenta simplified retro-reflector comb/E witness with one vertical spine and three horizontal bars.
verdicts.expected: RETRFL02 is retro reflector, simplified: magenta comb/E witness with vertical spine and horizontal bars.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: correct reflector family, colour, and simplified comb/E shape. Candidate is visually close enough for pending-human status; no final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.

verdicts.observed: Candidate is a magenta segmented/dashed circular ring and no longer a diamond placeholder.
verdicts.expected: RTPBCN02 is a radar transponder beacon: magenta segmented/ring glyph around the point.
verdicts.judge_comments: Semantic gate passes: the radar transponder beacon ring/segmented-circle family and magenta colour are preserved. The S-101 witness uses discrete arc segments rather than a dasharray circle, but this is acceptable Helm styling for pending-human status. No final approval implied.
verdicts.required_change: No repair required for this visual/semantic rerun; candidate may move only to judge_pass_pending_final_approval and must not be final-approved by this artifact.
